Nuclear is prominent in Genesis Mission projects
items.summary: The Department of Energy announced yesterday that it has selected 278 projects under the Genesis Mission request for applications, which opened in March.  According to a White House press release on the same day, $5 billion in federal commitments will be put toward the Genesis Mission.

items.title: Federal Court Upholds NRC’s Generic Severe-Accident Review for Nuclear Plant License Renewals
items.summary: A federal appeals court has upheld the Nuclear Regulatory Commission’s (NRC’s) use of a generic environmental analysis for nuclear plant license renewals, rejecting claims that the agency failed to adequately consider how aging components and climate change could affect the risk of severe reactor ac

items.title: Developers, officials talk advanced reactor outlook with NRC

items.title: NNSA green-lights Calciner Project decommissioning work at Y-12
items.summary: The National Nuclear Security Administration has granted authorization to Consolidated Nuclear Security (CNS) to begin the decontamination and decommissioning work with the Calciner Project (CALP) at the Y-12 National Security Complex, in Oak Ridge, Tenn.

items.title: Korean-US partnership for floating SMR platform
items.summary: South Korean shipbuilding company Samsung Heavy Industries announced it has signed a memorandum of understanding with USA-based engineering firm Sargent & Lundy for the development of a standardised floating small modular reactor platform.

GAO report describes cleanup progress at Moab Mill Site
items.summary: A new report released by the U.S. Government Accountability Office states that the Department of Energy’s Office of Environmental Management (EM) has disposed of more than 16 million tons of radioactive and hazardous waste from the Moab Mill Site. items.title: ACRS seeking new committee member
items.summary: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission’s Advisory Committee on Reactor Safeguards (ACRS) is currently seeking qualified candidates to fill an open committee member position. Applications for the position will be accepted until July 30. The ACRS is the world’s oldest organization devoted to reactor safety

items.title: Multiple MSRs proposed for French industrial park
items.summary: French molten salt reactor developer Stellaria has signed a memorandum of understanding with Unitel Group and Energie Hub to develop an integrated energy hub in the Fos-sur-Mer area of southern France.

items.title: Two more Czech sites lined up for Rolls-Royce SMRs
items.summary: The Czech Ministry of Industry and Trade, ČEZ and Rolls-Royce SMR have signed a memorandum of understanding to start preparatory activities for small modular reactor projects at two more locations.

items.summary: Researchers from Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory and Pacific Fusion have surpassed 3,000 “shots” with Sirius, a four-stage prototype impedance-matched Marx generator (IMG) of the type that can provide pulsed power to an inertial confinement fusion machine. items.summary: Slovenské elektrárne has said the assembly and hermetic sealing of the reactor has been completed and Slovakia's Mochovce unit 4 is ready to move on to the physical tests leading to the first controlled fission reaction.

items.title: Bulgaria's planned AP1000s 'strategic bilateral priority'
items.summary: Commitment to the on-going project to build two new units at the Kozloduy Nuclear Power Plant has been stressed in talks between senior Bulgarian and US representatives.

items.title: Experiments at the nation's only all-digital nuclear reactor
Spanish regulator supports Almaraz licence extension
items.summary: Spain's Nuclear Safety Council has issued a favourable report on the renewal of the operating licence for the two-unit Almaraz nuclear power plant. It says the plant meets the conditions to operate safely until June 2030.

items.title: Research Project Applies AI to Isotope Hydrology to Strengthen Water Management

items.title: Strengthening Viet Nam’s Nuclear Liability Framework
Siting licence granted for new Kola nuclear power plant units
items.summary: Russia's nuclear regulatory Rostekhnadzor has issued site licences for the first two proposed units at the new Kola Nuclear Power Plant.

items.title: IAEA condemns reported killing of chief engineer at Zaporizhzhia NPP
items.summary: The International Atomic Energy Agency said its Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi "condemns the reported incident which he says represents an unacceptable attack on the plant and its management, seriously threatening nuclear safety".

items.title: Update 358 – IAEA Director General Statement on Situation in Ukraine
Newcleo sets out plan for US licensing of reactor
items.summary: France-headquartered innovative nuclear energy company Newcleo has submitted a Regulatory Engagement Plan to the US Nuclear Regulatory Commission for the LFR-AS-200, the commercial 200 MWe version of its lead-cooled fast neutron reactor.

items.title: IAEA Draws Safeguards Conclusions for 179 States – IAEA Report
Nuclear Science is Transforming Rainbow Trout Farming in Latin America
Reactor vessel installed at Lufeng unit 1
items.summary: The reactor pressure vessel has been installed at unit 1 of the Lufeng nuclear power plant in China's Guangdong province, China General Nuclear announced.

items.title: Building new Swiss nuclear power plant economically viable, report says
items.summary: The construction of a new nuclear power plant in Switzerland would be economically viable, according to a study carried out by the BAK Economics research institute on behalf of the Swiss business federation Economiesuisse.

items.title: Third reactor at Changjiang plant starts up
items.summary: Unit 3 at the Changjiang nuclear power plant in China's Hainan province has attained a sustained chain reaction for the first time, China National Nuclear Corporation has announced.

items.title: IAEA Concludes International Physical Protection Advisory Follow-Up Mission in China
NRC exempts Westinghouse from design certification renewal rule
items.summary: The US Nuclear Regulatory Commission has said Westinghouse can apply to renew the AP1000 standard design certification, incorporating lessons learned from the construction and operation of Vogtle units 3 and 4, well ahead of the expiration of the current certificate.

items.title: IAEA-Led Mission Sample ALPS-Treated Water at Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Station under Add…
First commercial nuclear-powered satellite launched
items.summary: Miami-based City Labs Inc announced that its Betavoltaic Orbital High-Reliability satellite - the world's first commercial nuclear-powered satellite and first nuclear CubeSat - was on board SpaceX Transporter-17, a rideshare mission launched on Tuesday.

items.title: IAEA Launches First Regional Radiation Protection Training Course for Caribbean States
items.summary: The IAEA has launched its first Postgraduate Educational Course (PGEC) on Radiation Protection and the Safety of Radiation Sources for Caribbean countries, marking an important milestone in strengthening radiation safety capacity across the region.

items.title: Argentina announces privately-financed SMR plan
items.summary: Argentina's government has said US-based Meitner Energy is planning to invest USD1.2 billion in the construction of a 300 MW small modular reactor at the Atucha site.
